notes: All 15 responses describe a generic crowded beach scene; NONE identify it as a Martin Handford 'Where's Waldo' illustration or locate any hidden characters (Waldo/Odlaw/Wizard Whitebeard) - the central task of the image is universally missed. Common cross-response patterns: (1) the large blue/white hovercraft on the right is never named as a hovercraft - it is either ignored or folded into 'a large blue/white ferry/boat'; (2) every response mislabels the red-and-white Punch and Judy booth as a 'beach hut'; (3) the submarine, inflatable dinghy, cargo ships, and waterskier are never mentioned; (4) t0.7|rep1 and t0.7|rep2 uniquely (and correctly) hint at the 'Where's Waldo' genre but do not commit or find characters. The empty t0.1|rep1 is a clear generation failure. Higher-temperature reps (t0.4, t0.7) tend to be slightly richer (more often mention horses, ice cream cart, land-on-horizon) but also more prone to embellishments (ocean liner, ice cream machine, pregnant woman, red sails, green/white huts). Per the dense-scene rule, minor unverified figures (beach balls, sandcastles, individuals with cameras) were NOT treated as firm hallucinations unless clearly wrong (green/white huts, red sails).
